entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.09
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.09 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q3 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration

 

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.403 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Service Marketplace.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Data Integration
    • Enable credential sharing for IBP and Generic ODATA connections
  • Microsoft Office Integration
    • Add-in for Microsoft Excel
      • Support offline mode for cell-based formulas | (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Data Integration

 

Enable credential sharing for IBP and Generic ODATA connections

Users can now allow per-user credentials when creating and sharing new data acquisition connections for SAP Integrated Business Planning and generic OData Services data sources.

 

Microsoft Office Integration

 

Add-in for Microsoft Excel

 

Supporting offline mode for custom cell-based formulas (get and set data)

There is a new mode for workbooks that allow keeping the values for custom functions (also when changing sheet layout) when the end user is not connected to SAP Analytics Cloud or does not have the add-in. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Q2 2024
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans. This blog post introduces the new features and enhancements of the SAP Analytics Cloud Q2 2024 Quarterly Release (QRC).

Join our SAP Product Experts in the video below as they demonstrate some of the features highlighted in Q2 2024.

 

 

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

 

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.401 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
Version 1.0.91 for SAP universe (UNX)
Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Important: With every quarterly release update, there are required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud via SNOTES. This will ensure that the SAP BW back and font ends are in sync. To learn which SNOTES are required for implementation, refer to the XML-File attached in SAP Note 2541557.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Service Marketplace.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Administrative-level control of sharing public and private files | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Central control over user profile settings for administrators | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Expiration of file-repository backups | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Enforce Purpose and Grant type by OAuth Client
  • Augmented Analytics
    • Just Ask
      • Just ask: natural-language query | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
        • Just Ask administrators can now assign Labels to searchable models
      • Predictive Planning
        • Predictive Planning – making prediction intervals visible in stories | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Business Content Delivery
    • Business content packages for finance, commercial, and workforce planning | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Data Exploration
    • Data analyzer – copy cells from table (Ctrl C) | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data analyzer – export to PDF with the option to fit columns to page width | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data analyzer – support for workspaces | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Calculations dialog: Syntax highlighting
    • Filter in the filter line on calculated measures
    • Embedded Dialog Story Filter Panel
    • Builder panel enhancements
    •  
  • Data Integration
    • Support for live data connections to SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Variable dialog – text configuration, settings panel, and more | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Support x.509 certificates for SAP Integrated Business Planning connection type
  • Data Modeling
    • Provision of the “unit” property type | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Turn on Optimized Scenario Composer of Analytical Engine
  • Enterprise Planning
    • Advanced Formulas - Link function enabled with analytic models as a source | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data Actions - story API to poll the execution status | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data Actions – selecting 'Show Only Leaves' in tracing table | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Multi Actions - access external API URL from a detailed dialog | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Multi Actions - supporting parameters from analytic models | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Plan Process Management - extended APIs for stories and analytical applications | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Plan Process Management - notifications management | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Plan Process Management – ability to include descendants when creating processes using the Calendar Wizard | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Planning Trigger – updated formatting options | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Version Management - dynamic expansion of planning area during data entry | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Blog)
    • Data Clearance in the Cross-Model Copy Step of Data Actions
    • Data Actions - Show Calendar Usages of a Data Action
  • Microsoft Office Integration
    • Add-in for Microsoft Excel
      • Refresh Selected Functions | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • Formatting of a static report in Microsoft Excel
      • Design Pane Header Re-design
      • Recent List in Open Dialog
      • Custom Function: GETVARIABLES
      • Support for SAP BW and SAP S/4HANA live models
      • Prompt Enhancements
      • Supporting offline mode for custom cell-based formulas (get and set data) in Microsoft Excel | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Mobile Analytics
    • Integrating KPI tiles in SAP Mobile Start | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Story Design
    • Dynamic font-size scaling in the device preview functionality | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Filtering by member dialog – improved value selection experience | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Optimized (unified) story experience – dynamic font scaling, batch export, and more | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • Enhance Exporting all Table Content to Excel
      • Enable batch export when exporting your story to PDF or PPTX
      • CSV and Excel Export Support for UQM Table
      •  
    • Rank with others – view top-N and bottom-N in comparison to other data points | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Repointing a model in a story | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Blog)
    • Table – precision in numeric values | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Table - totals used in formula bar calculations as an anchor or as an operand | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Vertical filter panel – page filter section | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Wild Card Filters (excludes SAP BW and SAP IBP)
    • New Formatting Options in Theme Preferences
    • Lite Viewer Incremental Updates
    • Support for waterfall chart based on an SAP BW two-structure
    • Warn Customers on Deprecation of Classic Design Experience
  • Story Extensibility
    • Composite widgets (phase two) | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
      • Composite Enhancement: Support Responsive Layout
      • Composites: Data source Repointing
    • Custom widget support for the universal model | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Blog)
    • Supporting bursting mode for data change insights | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Section Widget Feature Gaps
  • Story Presentation
    • Unified story presentation mode | (SAP Road Map Explorer) (Blog)

 

Business Content Delivery

 

Business content packages for finance, commercial, and workforce planning

Reduce the time spent implementing SAP Analytics Cloud by adopting new, prebuilt data models and stories.

Updated packages for industries and lines of business for Q2 2024 include: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

New industry and line-of-business content packages:

  • Commercial planning
  • Consensus net revenue planning
  • Cross-model functionality for integrated financial planning for SAP S/4HANA
  • Group financial planning for SAP S/4HANA
  • Integrated financial planning for SAP S/4HANA
  • Operational workforce planning
  • Accessibility templates for SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Trade management
  • Order management suite
  • SAP for Utilities
  • SAP Risk and Assurance Management

Lear more about SAP Analytics Business Content here.

 

Data Exploration

 

Data analyzer – copy cells from table (Ctrl C)

This new feature supports the copy operation from Data Analyzer grid. Using keyboard shortcut Ctrl+C the user should be able to copy the selected cells and header information in raw format and paste to desired target supports data. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Enterprise Planning

 

Data Actions - story API to poll the execution status

Script API to proactively query the data action status: (SAP Road Map Explorer)

  • Return the data action status based on specific the execution ID
  • Establish execution dependencies based on dependent data action completion

 

Plan Process Management – ability to include descendants when creating processes using the Calendar Wizard

Enhancement to the Calendar Wizard task generation, enabling automatically generate events for child members when selecting a parent member of the driving dimension (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. For more details on these features, please visit the SAP Analytics Cloud Help Portal. You can also visit the SAP Road Map Explorer to see the upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: SAP 分析云 2024.09 版功能更新
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能 (BI)、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。

本文介绍了 SAP 分析云 2024.09 版本的新功能。这些新功能已经在 SAP 分析云 FastTrack 客户的系统上线。对于 SAP 分析云季度发布周期 (QRC) 客户，此版本及其功能将作为 QRC 2024 年第3季度版本的一部分提供。这里可以查看 QRC 发布时间表。

 

系统和技术要求


尽管不是必需的，我们推荐您升级到最新的 SAP 分析云代理版本 1.0.403，以利用所有数据采集类型。

您的代理需要1.0.75或以上版本，但以下情况除外：

  • SAP ERP/SQL 数据库版本1.0.99
  • SAP Universe (UNX)版本1.0.365（ SAP分析云代理需要额外的设置步骤，有关详细信息，请参阅 SAP 说明3262098）
  • SAP Business Warehouse (BW)版本1.0.353
  • SAP HANA View版本1.0.235
  • File Server版本1.0.248

更多详细信息，请参阅系统要求和技术先决条件。

关于SAP BW 与 SAP 分析云集成的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2541557 以了解更多详细信息。

有关通过 SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 与 SAP 分析云来实时访问 Universe 时的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2771921 以了解更多详细信息。

请从“Service Marketplace”下载最新版本的“简易部署工具箱”。

  • 为避免下载旧版本，以及可能让用户面对 Cloud Connector 与 SAPJVM 或 Apache Tomcat 的不当绑定问题，只有三个最新版本可供下载。

关于云代理的版本变更管理细节，请参考 SAP Note 3264839。
 

更新亮点

  • 数据集成
    • 为 IBP 和通用 ODATA 连接启用凭据共享
  • Microsoft Office 集成
    • Microsoft Excel 加载项
      • 支持基于单元格的公式的离线模式 | (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

 

 

数据集成

为 IBP 和通用 ODATA 连接启用凭据共享

现在用户可以在为 SAP Integrated Business Planning 和通用 OData 服务数据源创建和共享新的数据获取连接时，允许将凭据同连接一同分享。

 

 

Microsoft Office 集成 - Microsoft Excel 插件 

支持自定义基于单元格的公式的离线模式（获取并设置数据）

在工作簿的一种新模式下，允许在终端用户未连接到SAP Analytics Cloud或没有添加插件时，也能保留自定义函数的值（即使更改了工作表布局）。 (SAP Road Map Explorer)

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和改进功能的高级概述。您可以在针对FastTrack客户的应用内帮助指南中找到有关这些功能的更多详细信息，如果您想了解更多关于FastTrack和季度发布周期发布的信息，请参阅这篇SAP知识库文章。

 

如果您对本博文的内容有任何疑问，可以在下方发表评论。如果您有具体的产品或技术问题，请在我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 问答论坛上提问。访问我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 社区页面，了解更多产品信息、卓越实践等。查看我们的 SAP 路线图浏览器，了解 SAP Analytics Cloud 即将推出的更多功能。

entry.title: Import Data Connection to SAP S/4HANA in SAP Analytics Cloud : Technical Configuration
entry.content:
In this blog we’ll discuss complete steps for Import data connection from S/4 hana on-premise system to SAP Analytics Cloud . The Operating system we are using on which S/4 Hana system is installed is SUSE Linux SP 15.

 

 

 

Here, Our Data source is S/4 Hana from which the SAP Analytics Cloud is connected using the SAP Analytics Cloud Agent and SAP Cloud Connector.

It is recommended that the Cloud Connector, SAP Analytics Cloud agent, and the SAP Java Connector (JCo) are installed together on a dedicated server, and not a personal computer. This helps to ensure that multiple users can use an import data connection without experiencing slowness or downtime.

The SAP Analytics Cloud Agent Simple Deployment Kit allows you to quickly get your import data connections working.

The simple deployment kit is only supported on Windows 64-bit operating systems. If you have a different server, such as Linux, you'll need to install and configure the agent manually.

If we have windows Environment, then the SAP Analytics Cloud Agent Simple Deployment Kit is preferred. More details can be found in the below link.

SAP Analytics Cloud Agent Simple Deployment Kit | SAP Help Portal

For Linux Environment we have to install SAP JVM, SAP Cloud Connector, Tomcat Apache server, SAC Agent, Java Connector.

Below are the steps for Import Data connection for On-premise S/4 HANA systems -

  1. SAP JVM 8.1 and JDK installation on the linux server
  2. Install SAP Cloud Connector on the same linux server.
  3. Installing Tomcat Apache server
  4. Installing and configuring Java connector (Jco)in Tomcat .
  5. Installing and configuring SAP Analytics Cloud Agent .
  6. Configuration and Connection steps in SAC portal

 

1. SAP JVM 8.1 installation on the same linux server

Download the SAP JVM from the below link:

https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#cloud

Download the rpm file compatible for linux 86x64 -
sapjvm-8.1.095-linux-x64.rpm (sha1)

Move the file to the server and run the below command to install SAP JVM

rpm -i <file_name>.rpm

 

Also it is recommended to install the openjdk packages on SUSE linux .

Run command ‘zypper install java’

 

To check the verison of openjdk installed.

Run command 'java –version'

2. Install SAP Cloud Connector on the same linux server.

To download the SAP Cloud Connector go to the below link :

https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/#cloud

 

Download the zip file for Linux x86_64 Architechture and move the downloaded file to the server.

Now, unzip the file and install the rpm package for cloud connector as below.

Cloud connector is installed now.

To open the SCC, enter: https://<hostname>:8443 in a browser, where the <hostname> is the hostname of the machine on which the connector is installed, and the port number is the one configured during installation. The default port number is 8443

By default Login Credentials are :

Username : Administrator

Password : manage

After the fist login , It prompts you to change password for the Administrator User.

Now Add the SAC subaccount to the cloud connector:

 Click on “Add subaccount” and enter the details SAC subaccount , which you can find on SAP Analytics Cloud.

To find the Subaccount details of SAP Analytics Cloud :

You must use a System Owner account to perform the following steps. If you don't know who the system owner is, log on to SAP Analytics Cloud and from the side navigation, choose  Security > Users. 

 

  • Log on to SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • From the side navigation, choose System  > Administration.
  • Switch to the Data Source Configuration tab.

 

3.Installing Tomcat  Apache Server :

 

  • Create a directory named tomcat in the /opt folder:
  • Go to the Apache Tomcat 8 Download page by clicking this https://tomcat.apache.org/download-80.cgi  Place your cursor under 8.5.65 Binary Distributions, right-click on the tar file and select the copy link address from the menu that appears (as shown in the picture below). At the time of writing, Tomcat 8 is the most recent edition, but you are free to use whatever version is most current.

  • Download the file from wget

  • Extract the tar.gz file

  • Starting Tomcat apache server

 

4.Installing and configuring Java connector (Jco)in Tomcat .

 

  • Download SAP Java connector from SAP support portal -> Software downloads

  • On a Linux server, put the sapjco3.jar and libsapjco3.so in the lib folder of Apache Tomcat.

 

5. Installing and configuring SAP Analytics Cloud Agent .

 

It is recommended, but not mandatory, that the Cloud Connector, SAP Analytics Cloud agent, and the SAP JCO are installed together on a dedicated server, and not a personal computer. This helps to ensure that multiple users can use an import data connection without experiencing slowness or downtime.

Modifying the Cloud Connector’s embedded web application and deploying the agent to it is not supported.

  • Download the SAP Analytics Cloud agent from the SAP Support Portal.

The agent is available on the SAP Software Downloads page. Expand By Category, select SAP Cloud Solutions > SAP ANALYTICS CLOUD CONN > SAP ANALYTICS CLOUD CONN 1.0 > SAP ANALYTICS CLOUD AGENT 1.0 and download the latest version.

  • Unzip the downloaded file and rename the WAR file to C4A_AGENT.war.
  • Extract the package and copy the file C4A_AGENT.war to your Tomcat webapps directory.

The agent will automatically deploy when Tomcat is restarted. 

  • Create a user for the SAP Analytics Cloud agent and assign the Services role to the user.

 

  • The user credentials will be needed later for setting up the connection to SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

  • Restart the Tomcat application server for the settings to take effect.
  • Test if the installation was successful, by opening the following URL in your browser: http://<Host>:<Port>/C4A_AGENT/deploymentInfo

The version of the SAP Analytics Cloud agent installed is displayed.

 

6. Configuration and Connection steps in SAC portal

 

  • Add the Data source in System > Administration > on-premise data sources

 

  • Check connection configuration , It should be green.

 

  • From the side navigation, choose 

     

    Connections >  (Add Connection).

The Select a data source dialog will appear.

  • Expand Acquire Data and select SAP S/4HANA.
  • In the New SAP S/4HANA Connection dialog, do the following:
    1. In the Connection Information section, add a Connection Name and Description.
    2. If you are connecting to an SAP S/4HANA on-premise system, select Connect to an On-Premise OData service, and then select the Location of your Cloud Connector from the list.
    3. Enter the Data Service URL published during your configuration
  • Select the authentication type.
  • Enter the User Name and Password of the user you want to import data from.
  • Choose Create.

The new connection is added to the list of connections on the Connections screen.

 

Conclusion :

Import Data Connection to S/4Hana on-premise system in SAP Analytics Cloud has been established successfully.

entry.title: Unleashing the Power of Custom Widgets in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Agenda:

  • Introduction
  • Prerequisites
  • Why custom widget?
  • Developing Custom Widget
  • Limitations
  • Conclusion

 

Dear SAP community,

In today's world where data is crucial, it's really important to have analytics solutions that fit our business needs perfectly. With SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), we can create custom analytical applications that are just right for our needs. One cool thing we can do with SAC is develop custom widgets. These widgets help us add more functions to SAC beyond what it already offers. In this blog post, I'm excited to talk about my experience making a custom SAC widget using a JSON file.

Prerequisites:

  1. Proficiency in JavaScript and JSON: Custom widgets in SAC are developed using JavaScript and defined using JSON files. Ensure that you have a good understanding of these languages and their syntax.
  2. Development Environment: Set up a development environment with the necessary tools for JavaScript development, such as a code editor (e.g., Visual Studio Code) and a web browser for testing.
  3. SAP Analytics Cloud Tenant: Access to an SAC tenant where you can create and test custom widgets. This may require coordination with your SAC administrator or IT department.
  4. Access to Documentation and Resources: Familiarize yourself with the official SAP documentation and resources related to custom widget development in SAC. This includes the SAC Developer Guide, API reference documentation, and any relevant tutorials or guides provided by SAP.
  5. Understanding of Widget Lifecycle and Events: Gain an understanding of the lifecycle of custom widgets in SAC, including initialization, rendering, data binding, and event handling. Understanding how widgets interact with the SAC environment will be crucial for developing robust and efficient solutions.
  6. Testing Environment: Set up a testing environment where you can deploy and test your custom widgets in a controlled environment before deploying them to production. This could be a sandbox or development environment within your SAC tenant.

 

 

Why Custom Widgets?

SAC provides a rich set of pre-built visualizations and functionalities. However, there are instances where users may require specialized visualizations or interactions that aren't available out-of-the-box. This is where custom widgets shine. By creating custom widgets, users can tailor their analytics applications to address unique business requirements, enhancing decision-making and driving business outcomes.

Developing a Custom Widget

Developing a custom widget in SAC is a straightforward process. It begins with defining the widget's properties and behaviours using a JSON file. This file specifies the widget's appearance, data binding, interactions, and any custom logic required. Once the JSON file is defined, it can be uploaded to SAC, where it is rendered as a fully-functional widget within the application.

During the development process, I found the documentation provided by SAP to be comprehensive and user-friendly, guiding me through each step with clarity. Additionally, the SAC community forums proved to be an invaluable resource, offering insights and solutions to common challenges encountered during widget development.

Step 1:

Download the git bash using the below commands

Step 2:

Download the git angular file using the below command in git bash

Step 3:

Go into your file location and execute “npm i”

step 4:

Once you installed the angular ,execute “node server.js” to enable localhost 3000

Step 5 :

Execute “ngrok http 3000”

 

Step 6:

Copy the forwarding link from output and add “static/googleGauge.png” and “static/googleGauge. js” and paste in 7th and 15th line like in the below image

 https://2ece-2401-4900-1cd1-b5dc-c1bf-2d2-c435-affd.ngrok-free.app/static/googleGauge.png

https://2ece-2401-4900-1cd1-b5dc-c1bf-2d2-c435-affd.ngrok-free.app/static/googleGauge.js

Step 7:

Search “Google gauge chart cdn” go to the link in the src from the below image and copy the js code

https://www.gstatic.com/charts/loader.js

Step 8:

Remove the selected lines in the below image and paste the code in googleGauge.js

Now your JSON file is ready.

Step 9:

Login to your SAC in Chrome/Edge **
And open analytical application.

 

 

Step 10:

Navigate to Custom widget and use that ‘+’ icon to add your JSON file.

Step 11:

Once your JSON file successfully imported, again navigate to analytical application and create a new analytical application.

Note**: As Classic Design Experience mode is deprecating, prefer optimised mode for better experience.

 

Step 12:

Go to insert -> custom widget -> choose your desire JSON file.

Step 13:
Once you added that JSON file the design you build in VS code will be replicated here. Then add input field, two buttons for plus and minus.

On-Initialise Code:

Minus Button Code:

Add Button Code:

Canvas Code:

Output:

I gave ‘123’ as Label and checked ‘+’, ‘-‘ buttons were working fine.

Limitations:

  1. Limited Browser Support: Custom widgets are rendered using web technologies such as HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, which may not be fully supported across all web browsers. Ensure compatibility with supported browsers, as specified by SAP, to avoid potential rendering issues for end-users.
  2. Performance Considerations: Custom widgets may introduce performance overhead, especially if they involve complex calculations or data processing. Be mindful of performance implications and optimize your custom widgets accordingly to ensure a smooth user experience.
  3. Dependency on SAC Updates: Custom widgets may rely on SAC APIs or underlying platform features, which could be subject to change with platform updates. Regularly review SAP's release notes and announcements to stay informed about any changes that may impact your custom widgets.
  4. Limited Debugging Tools: Debugging custom widgets can be challenging, as SAC provides limited debugging tools compared to traditional web development environments. Utilize browser developer tools and logging mechanisms within your custom widgets to aid in troubleshooting and debugging.
  5. Security Considerations: Custom widgets execute within the SAC environment and have access to sensitive data. Ensure that your custom widgets adhere to best practices for security, such as data encryption, input validation, and protection against common web vulnerabilities like cross-site scripting (XSS) and injection attacks.
  6. Lack of Official Support for Third-Party Libraries: While SAC allows you to use third-party libraries within custom widgets, SAP does not officially support or guarantee compatibility with all libraries. Exercise caution when incorporating third-party dependencies and thoroughly test their compatibility with SAC.
  7. Limited Accessibility Features: SAC custom widgets may lack built-in accessibility features, such as keyboard navigation and screen reader support. Ensure that your custom widgets adhere to accessibility standards to provide an inclusive user experience for all users.
  8. Development Complexity: Developing custom widgets requires proficiency in web technologies such as JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. Additionally, understanding SAC's widget API and platform-specific nuances adds to the development complexity. Invest time in learning and mastering these technologies to effectively develop and maintain custom widgets.

Conclusion

In conclusion, the ability to develop custom widgets within SAP Analytics Cloud empowers users to create tailored analytics solutions that drive business value. By leveraging the flexibility and extensibility of custom widgets, organizations can unlock new insights, streamline analytics processes, and ultimately, make better decisions.

I encourage fellow SAP Community members to explore the possibilities of custom widgets within SAC and share their experiences and insights. Together, we can continue to innovate and elevate the power of analytics in the digital age.

Reference Link:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/file-upload-widget-how-to-develop-custom-widgets-with-the-react-framework/ba-p/13572666

Tags: SAP Analytics Cloud, hybrid analytics  SAP Analytics Cloud #SAC_Customwidget  #HANA 

 

Happy Analyzing!

Akash Kannan K
Associate Consultant, SAP DATA ANALYST

entry.title: SAP分析云2024年第二季度更新介绍
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能 (BI)、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。

本文介绍 SAP 分析云 2024 年第二季度的季度发布 (QRC) 的新功能和增强功能。

在链接的视频中，SAP 产品专家将向您展示 2024 年第二季度的部分重点功能。

 

系统和技术要求

尽管不是必需的，我们推荐您升级到最新的 SAP 分析云代理版本 1.0.401，以利用所有数据采集类型。

您的代理需要1.0.75或以上版本，但以下情况除外：

  • SAP ERP/SQL 数据库版本1.0.99
  • SAP Universe (UNX)版本1.0.91
  • SAP Business Warehouse (BW)版本1.0.353
  • SAP HANA View版本1.0.235
  • File Server版本1.0.248

更多详细信息，请参阅系统要求和技术先决条件。

每季度发布更新时，都需要通过 SNOTES 更新 SAP BW 与 SAP 分析云的集成。这将确保 SAP BW 的后端和前端同步。要了解实施时需要哪些 SNOTES，请参阅 SAP Note 2541557 中所附的 XML 文件。

关于SAP BW 与 SAP 分析云集成的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2541557 以了解更多详细信息。

有关通过 SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 与 SAP 分析云来实时访问 Universe 时的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2771921 以了解更多详细信息。

请从“Service Marketplace”下载最新版本的“简易部署工具箱”。

  • 为避免下载旧版本，以及可能让用户面对 Cloud Connector 与 SAPJVM 或 Apache Tomcat 的不当绑定问题，只有三个最新版本可供下载。

关于云代理的版本变更管理细节，请参考 SAP Note 3264839。

 

更新亮点

  • 系统和服务管理
    • 共享公共文件和私有文件的管理级别控制 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 管理员对用户配置文件设置的集中控制 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 文件资源库的备份到期设置 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 按 OAuth 客户端执行目的和授权类型 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
  • 增强分析
    • “Just Ask”：自然语言查询 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
      • “Just Ask”管理员现在可以将标签分配到可搜索模型
    • 计划预测
      • 计划预测 - 在故事中展示预测区间 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
  • 业务内容交付
    • 面向财务、商业和劳动力计划的业务内容包 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
  • 数据发掘
    • 数据分析器－从表中复制单元格（Ctrl C）|（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 数据分析器－导出为 PDF 时设置适合页面宽度 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 数据分析器－数据分析器中支持工作区 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 计算对话框：语法突出显示
    • 在筛选行中对计算出的量值进行筛选 
    • 嵌入式对话故事筛选面板 
    • 生成器面板功能改进 
  • 数据集成
    • 支持面向供应链的 SAP 集成业务计划的实时数据连接 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 变量对话框－文本配置、设置面板等 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 支持 SAP Integrated Business Planning 连接类型的 x.509 证书 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
  • 数据建模
    • 提供“单位”属性类型 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 启用分析引擎的优化方案编写器
  • 企业计划
    • 高级公式 – 分析模型作为源，使用链接函数 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 数据操作－用于轮询执行状态的故事 API |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 数据操作 - 在跟踪表中选择“仅显示叶项” | （SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 多步操作 – 从对话框中复制外部API的URL等信息  | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 多步操作 – 在多步操作参数中支持分析模型 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计划流程管理 - 用于故事和分析应用的扩展API | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计划流程管理 - 通知管理 | （SAP 路线图浏览器）
    • 计划流程管理 – 使用日历向导创建流程时能够包括子节点 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 计划触发器－更新了格式设置选项 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 版本管理 - 数据输入过程中动态扩展规划区域 | （SAP 路线图浏览器） | （博文）
    • 数据操作中跨模型复制步骤的数据清理
    • 数据操作 - 新增了日历用法的显示
  • Microsoft Office 集成
    • Microsoft Excel 加载项
      • 刷新选定功能 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
      • Microsoft Excel 中静态报表的格式设置
      • 重新设计设计窗格标题
      • 打开对话框中的最近使用列表
      • 自定义函数：GETVARIABLES
      • 支持 SAP BW 和 SAP S/4HANA 实时模型
      • 提示功能改进
      • 支持 Microsoft Excel 中基于自定义单元格公式（获取和设置数据）的离线模式 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
  • 移动分析
    • 在 SAP Mobile Start 中集成 KPI 磁贴 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
  • 故事设计
    • 设备预览功能中支持动态字体大小缩放 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 按成员筛选对话框 - 改进了值选择体验 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 优化（统一）故事体验 - 动态字体缩放、批量导出等 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
      • 增强将所有表内容导出到 Excel的功能
      • 将故事导出为 PDF 或 PPTX 时启用批量导出
      • 支持UQM 表的 CSV 和 Excel 导出
    • “其他”序列功能 - 查看其他数据点的前 N 项和后 N 项 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 在故事中重新指向模型 | （SAP路线图浏览器）|（博客）
    • 表 - 数值精度 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 表 - “总计”作为客户端计算的锚点或操作数 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 垂直筛选器面板 - 页面筛选器部分 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 通配符筛选器（不包括 SAP BW 和 SAP IBP）
    • 主题首选项中的新格式选项
    • 精简查看器的增量更新
    • 支持基于 SAP BW 二级结构的瀑布图
    • 提醒客户经典设计体验停用
  • 故事可扩展性
    • 组合微件（第二阶段） | （SAP路线图浏览器）
      • 综合增强：支持响应式布局
      • 组合：数据源重新定向
    • 通用模型的自定义微件支持 | （SAP路线图浏览器）|（博客）
    • 支持数据变化洞察的群发模式 | （SAP路线图浏览器）
    • “区段”微件功能弥补差距
  • 故事演示
    • 统一故事演示模式 | （SAP路线图浏览器）|（博客）

 

业务内容交付

面向财务、商业和劳动力计划的业务内容包

采用新的预构建数据模型和故事，减少实施 SAP 分析云时所花费的时间。

2024 年第 2 季度针对行业和业务线更新的软件包如下：（SAP路线图浏览器）

新的行业和业务线内容包：

  • 商业计划
  • 共同点净收入计划
  • 面向 SAP S/4HANA 的集成财务计划的跨模型功能
  • 面向 SAP S/4HANA 的组财务计划
  • 面向 SAP S/4HANA 的集成财务计划
  • 运营劳动力计划
  • SAP 分析云的可访问性模板
  • 贸易管理
  • 订单管理套件
  • SAP for Utilities
  • SAP 风险和保障管理

点击此处 ，了解 SAP 业务分析内容的更多信息。

 

数据发掘

数据分析器－从表中复制单元格（Ctrl C）

此新功能支持从数据分析器网格中进行复制操作。使用键盘快捷键 Ctrl + C，用户可以复制选定单元格和标题信息的原始格式，之后粘贴到所需目标支持数据。（SAP路线图浏览器）

 

企业计划

数据操作－用于轮询执行状态的故事 API

用于主动查询数据操作状态的脚本 API：（SAP路线图浏览器）

  • 根据特定的执行 ID 返回数据操作状态
  • 基于相关数据操作完成情况建立执行依赖关系

 

计划流程管理 – 使用日历向导创建流程时能够包括子节点

增强日历向导任务生成，在选择动因维的父成员时自动为子成员生成事件（SAP路线图浏览器）

 

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和增强功能的高级概述。有关这些功能的更多详细信息，请访问 SAP 分析云帮助门户。您还可以访问 SAP 路线图浏览器查看 SAP 分析云即将推出的功能

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.10
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.10 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q3 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

 

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.405 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Software Center.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Administrative-level control of sharing public and private files | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Augmented Analytics
    • Forecast in chart support in optimized story experience
  • Enterprise Planning
    • Advanced Formulas – supporting the “any” hierarchy parameter | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Data Actions – migration of existing allocation design time | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
  • Story Design
    • Descriptions for story calculations | (SAP Road Map Explorer)
    • Improved Linked Analysis with Visualization Drill

 

Administration and Service Management

 

Administrative-level control of sharing public and private files

Sharing privilege have been added in the Roles page on public and private files. This change allows the system administrator to enable or disable a user’s ability to share content that is saved in private or public folders. The rule will apply to the owner of a file as well. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Augmented Analytics

 

Forecast in chart support in optimized story experience

Users can now add a forecast to time series or line charts in the optimized experience (view and design mode).

 

Enterprise Planning

 

Advanced Formulas – supporting the “any” hierarchy parameter

When designing advanced formulas, users can now include a data action parameter that has ANY type hierarchy, without the need to specify a hierarchy for the dimension the parameter is set on using CONFIG.HIERARCHY. This provides more flexibility by choosing the hierarchy to use when running the data action containing the advanced formulas. Note a parameter of date dimension with its hierarchy set to Any cannot be included. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Data Actions – migration of existing allocation design time

Legacy allocations are currently only available to view and execute. Existing legacy allocations have been automatically migrated to data actions in the folder “Structured Allocations” on system upgrade. The legacy allocation designer has been disabled. New allocations can be created in the data action designer.

 

Story Design

 

Descriptions for story calculations

In the optimized story experience, when creating calculations and calculation input controls in the calculation editor, user can now provide both a Name (ID) and Description. If a Description is not provided, the ID will be used instead. This enhancement allows for creating multiple calculations with the same description. (SAP Road Map Explorer)

 

Improved Linked Analysis with Visualization Drill

In the optimized story experience, drilling up on a chart after drilling down on multiple members will now show the same results whether or not Linked Analysis is enabled.

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find mor details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases.

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog, you can comment below. If you have product specific of technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Integration Between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud - Blog 1
entry.content:
Introduction

Hello all and welcome to the first segment of this five-part series 👋
 
This blog series discusses the integration between SAP Datasphere with SAP Analytics Cloud for data harmony. I aim to provide an overview of SAC, discuss the process of establishing both live and OData connections between SAC and Datasphere, explore Optimized Design Experience, delve into the current planning capabilities bridging these two products, and more.
 
In this introductory blog, I want to provide an overview of the following key aspects for integrating data between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud:

  • Understanding SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

  • Exploring the advantages of SAC compared to other reporting tools.

  • Elevating your data connection experience - guidance on setting up the data connection for optimal functionality.

 

Understanding SAP Analytics Cloud

SAP Analytic Cloud (SAC) is a cloud-based solution developed by SAP. It offers a comprehensive and robust suite of analytics capabilities. This allows businesses to analyze, visualize, plan, and predict data across various sources in real-time, including SAP Datasphere.

 
 

Why SAC over any Other Reporting Tool? The Unrivalled Champion of Analytics

  • SAC integrates seamlessly with the SAP Ecosystem. If your business is heavily invested in the SAP ecosystem, SAC offers tighter integration and support compared to other tools. Users can leverage their existing infrastructure (SAP HANA, SAP BW, and SAP S/4HANA etc.) This makes SAC a more natural choice for SAP-centric environments.

  • SAC eliminates the need for multiple tools. Unlike other analytic tools, SAC provides a unified, “all-in-one” platform for analytics, planning and predictive capabilities.

  • SAC provides an intuitive UI with easy-to-use drag-and-drop functionality. For those with more technical expertise, SAC allows for scripting capabilities.

 

Elevating Your Data Connection Experience

The first step we need to take for working with data from Datasphere within SAC, is setting up the connection. This connection will act as the link or ‘handshake’ between the two systems. We want to connect Datasphere as a remote live connection to our SAC tenant.

 

Step 1. Add Datasphere and SAC as ‘Trusted Origins’ with each other.

This security-based concept establishes a trusted relationship between the two platforms. This enables secure communication and data exchange, ensuring that requests and responses between Datasphere and SAC are authenticated and authorized. This in return, enhances overall data integrity and protection. To do this, go to System >> Administration >> App Integration and simply paste the URL from the other tenant in the Trusted Origins space.

 

 

Step 2. Configure the link between the two tenants.

When you link your tenants, you’ll be able to easily navigate between the two tenants. You’ll be able to switch between Datasphere and SAC in the top right-hand corner of the screen. Configuring the link between the two tenants fosters collaboration, efficiency, and a unified user experience for businesses leveraging these platforms. Go to System >> System Configuration >> Link My Tenants

 

 

Once this functionality has been properly configured, users gain the ability to effortlessly navigate between the connected tenants by utilizing the Product Switch menu located in the top shell bar, as illustrated below. If the user is working on a project using both tenants, the Product Switch menu optimizes their productivity and reduces the time required for task-switching. Overall, it contributes to a smoother and more intuitive UI, ultimately enhancing user satisfaction and productivity.

 

 

Conclusion

In this blog series introduction, we've highlighted the seamless integration between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). The benefits of choosing SAC, its intuitive and easy-to-follow UI, and the key steps for setting up the data connection have been outlined. Keep an eye out as we continue to explore and unlock the full potential of SAC and Datasphere for enhanced data and analytics solutions!

 

SAP Datasphere SAP Analytics Cloud Data and Analytics SAP Analytics Cloud, connectivity

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP Integrated Business Planning
entry.content:
The objective of this blog post is to help provide the knowledge around establishing the live data connection between SAP Analytics Cloud and the SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) system.  

This allows the SAP Analytics Cloud users to leverage this connectivity to directly plug with SAP IBP system with SAC and run real-time analytics on top of the SAP IBP data. This means that, any changes made to the SAP IBP data will be immediately reflected in the SAC stories and models using this new live connection. The SAC users can also leverage the ‘cross-data analytics’ as the single SAC story in the SAC enterprise system could now be created based on the data coming from multiple data sources such as SAP HANA, SAP S/4 HANA, SAP BW, and many more data sources.  This of course now includes SAP IBP.   

Check here for an overview around the several data connections offered by SAC. Below is the architecture for the SAC live data connection to an SAP IBP system using Direct connection type.  
 

 

Pre-requisites: 

  • System details and the login credentials to the SAP Integrated Business Planning system.   
  • Setup SSO (optional): If you want users to have a single sign-on experience to your data, check you are using same Identity Provider (IdP) for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP NetWeaver. For more information on setting up your identity provider in SAP Analytics Cloud, see Enable a Custom SAML Identity Provider.  

 

Steps to create the live connection:  

  1. On IBP, create a new communication system using the Communication Systems app.  For the technical details, enter the host name of the SAP Analytics Cloud system with schema xxx.sapanalytics.cloud. 

 

  1. Create a new communication arrangement using the Communication Arrangements app. Use communication scenario SAP_COM_0065. Maintain the tenant ID of the SAP Analytics Cloud system.  This can be found in the system URL of the SAC system.  For example: 

https://xxx.sapanalytics.cloud/sap/fpa/ui/tenants/«tenant ID»/app.html. 

 

The full details on the IBP configuration steps can be found in the SAP IBP2405 documentation under: Administration -> Communication Management -> Communication Scenarios in SAP IBP -> SAP Analytics Cloud Integration 

Creating the live connection in the SAP Analytics Cloud: 

1. From the side navigation, choose   Connections  ->   (Add Connection).  

The Select a data source dialog will appear.  

2. Expand Connect to Live Data and select SAP Integrated Business Planning.  

3. In the dialog, enter a name and description for your connection.  

The connection name cannot be changed later.  

4. Add your SAP Integrated Business Planning host name.  

5. HTTPS Port is optional.   

6. (Optional) Choose a Default Language from the list.  

This language will always be used for this connection and cannot be changed by users without administrator privileges.  

7. Under Authentication Method it is by default selected as ‘SAML Single Sign On (Standard-Compliant)   

8. Select OK.  

Note: After creating a connection to a remote system and before creating a model from a remote system, you must log off and log on to SAP Analytics Cloud again.  

Please watch this video to find more details. 

Conclusion:  
You can configure a successful live connection between SAP Analytics Cloud and the SAP Integrated Business Planning systems by following the above steps.

entry.title: Integration Between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud - Blog 3
entry.content:
Introduction 

Dia duit 👋and welcome to the third instalment of this five-part blog series.
 
Previously, in blog 2 we discussed the following: 

  • Crafting compelling stories and visual masterpieces 

  • Embrace the future with Optimized Design Experience (ODE) 

 

In this blog, I am going to share with you the steps we took to create a live connection between Datasphere and SAC. Creating a live connection between Datasphere and SAC is crucial for improving data analysis capabilities and support better decision-making. Having the live connection between the two platforms allows for real-time data analysis meaning users can access the most up-to-date information for their analysis, leading to more accurate insights.

 

These steps include:

 

  1. Add Datasphere and SAC as ‘Trusted Origins’ with each other

  2. Create the live connection in SAC

  3. Create a SAC story & test your connection

 

Add Datasphere and SAC as ‘Trusted Origins’ with each other

This step has been discussed in depth under the ‘Elevating Your Data Connection Experience’ in blog 1 of this blog series. Just to give a quick overview, trusted origins is security-based concept that establishes a trusted relationship between the two platforms. It allows secure communication and data exchange, ensuring that requests and responses between Datasphere and SAC are authenticated and authorized.

 
 

Create the Live Connection

By creating a live connection between Datasphere and SAC, users can build stories directly against the data that is modelled in Datasphere. Analytical Datasets must be exposed for consumption first in Datasphere. If you've established a live connection but can't find the data you need when creating your story, ensure that it's deployed and configured for use within your Datasphere system.

 

In SAC, go to connections an create a new connection. Expand the ‘Connect to Live Data’ and select SAP Datasphere.

 

 

 

When you select SAP Datasphere, you’ll you asked to enter the Datasphere Host and HTTPS Port. You’ll also be asked to give your live connection a name. Where to find the Host & HTTPS Port?
 
 

 

 

Your Datasphere host name can be found from your browser’s address bar when logged on to your Datasphere tenant. The HTTPS port is always 443.
 

 

 

SAML Single Sign On is preselected as the authentication method allowed. When you click on OK, you'll be prompted to log on to Datasphere. Once you enter valid username and password the connection will be successfully set up.
 

 

 

Create a SAC story & test that your connection is successful

Once the connection is successfully created, the users can directly access and analyze the data from Datasphere without the need to duplicate modelling efforts or create a separate model within SAC. To learn more about this step, refer to blog 2 of this blog series under the section titled 'Crafting Compelling Stories and Visual Masterpieces'.
 

 

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, establishing a live connection between Datasphere and SAC is pivotal for enhancing data analysis capabilities and facilitating better decision-making. By following the outlined steps, including adding both platforms as trusted origins, creating the live connection in SAC, and testing its success, users can seamlessly access and analyze real-time data from Datasphere within SAC.

entry.title: Integration Between SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud - Blog 4
entry.content:
Introduction 

Hello and welcome to the fourth instalment of this five-part series. Previously we discussed: 

  • Crafting compelling stories and visual masterpieces
  • Embrace the future with Optimized Design Experience (ODE)

 

At the moment, there are 2 ways to integrate Datasphere with SAC for Planning Purposes:

  1. Sending data from Datasphere to SAC via OData Service
  2. Sending data from SAC to Datasphere via CDI Connection (Cloud Data Integration)

 

In this blog, I want to provide the steps we followed to create an OData Service Connection to send data from Datasphere to SAC. By integrating Datasphere Actuals into SAC, we lay the foundation for strategic decision-making, driving business growth and agility.


Sending Data from Datasphere to SAC via OData Service

 

 

Our goal for this task is to consume actuals from Datasphere into SAC for reporting and planning purposes. We will combine the actuals from Datasphere with planning data in SAC.

Here is a list of steps we took to import Datasphere actual data into SAC:

1. Identify the OData Source (Data Service URL) in Datasphere

In order for us the identify the Data Service URL we went to the following link. Within this URL, you can see all the assets available within the spaces available in your Datasphere tenant. 

 

Within this URL, we copied the assetRelationDataURL for the dataset we required. This is your Data Service URL. We then looked up our chosen assetRelationDataURL using the URL in the figure below. This will get us the meta data from the OData Service.

NOTE: To view the data from your chosen asset, add the dataset name again at the end of the URL.

2. Lookup your redirect URI in SAC

The redirect URI in SAC is the specific endpoint URL. The URI is the destination to which the authentication server redirects the user's browser after authentication has been successfully completed.

We added an OData Services connection in our SAC tenant. We then change the Authentication Type to OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code. At the bottom of this window, we can see the Redirect URI.

 

3. Create the OAuth Client in Datasphere

We now want to create an OAuth client in Datasphere. We need the Redirect URI from the above step to create the OAuth client. We do this by going to System >> Administration >> App Integration and click on the ‘Add a New OAuth Client’. Once you paste the Redirect URI and click Add, you’ll get a list of the following information which you will need to take a note of.

  • OAuth Client ID
  • Secret
  • Authorization URL
  • Token URL

 

4. Create OData Connection in SAC

Within SAC, we go to Connections >> Add a Connection and chose OData Service. You need to now add the following connection parameters:

  • Data Service URL = assetRelationalDataUrl
  • OAuth Client ID
  • Secret
  • Token URL 
  • Authorisation URL

5. Import from OData Connection

We now need to create a model in SAC. We chose OData Services as the data source and click the new connection we just made. We then create a new query and select which dimensions and measures you want when building that query.

 

You can see that we have now consumed actuals data from datasphere to SAC! At this stage you can clean data and fix any issues. You have successfully imported the actuals data from OData Connection!!

 

6. Create Story and Test

As you can see here, we created a story. We added a table to our story. We chose the query we created above as the data source for the table. We added ‘Category’ as a column and using the version management functionality we can choose both the Actual and Plan category. This shows Actual data from Datasphere and Plan data within SAC.

 

Conclusion

 

To recap, we have successfully sent Actual data from Datasphere to SAC via OData Service. To do this we identified the data service URL in Datasphere. Using this and other information we created an OAuth Client. We then completed the cycle by creating the OData Connection in SAC. Once we have successfully created the OData connection, we created a story and tested to see the Actual data from Datasphere in SAC.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.11
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.11 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q3 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

 

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.407 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Software Center.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Prevent loss of SAP Analytics Cloud tenant Accidentally Deleted via BTP
  • Data Exploration
    • Add Calculations on Structure Members for two Structure BW Queries in Data Analyzer
    • Persistence of last used values in Data Analyzer
  • Data Integration
    • Data Export API - Delta Difference
    • Support X509 certificate auth for OData-based Connections
  • Story Design
    • Optimized Story Experience now supports Export as Google Slides
    • Lite Viewer now Supports Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension in Geographical Visualizations
  • Story Extensibility
    • Retrieving non-input-ready variables as dynamic text or through script API

 

Administration and Service Management

 

Prevent loss of SAP Analytics Cloud tenant Accidentally Deleted via BTP

If you accidentally delete your service instance in the SAP BTP cockpit, it can be restored within seven days. Learn more here.

 

Data Exploration

 

Add Calculations on Structure Members for two Structure BW Queries in Data Analyzer

A "+Add Calculations" button has been added in the Structure section of the Available Objects panel. This allows the creation of calculations using structure members in two structured queries on BW.

 

Persistence of last used values in Data Analyzer

The last used values for defined components and interactions are now kept so that when data analyzer is reopened, the options are the same. That way as a data analyzer user you can now set the following:

  • Show/hide the filter line by default
  • Open/close individually the styling panel and the builder panel & available objects list 
  • Expand/collapse the styling panel and the builder panel & available objects list by default
  • Set the system and connection type by default in the data source browser
  • Keep all settings in the individual export dialogs for PDF, Microsoft Excel and CSV export (except for Export name)

 

Data Integration

 

Data Export API - Delta Difference

Data Export Service API subscriptions has been extended to support the calculation of the difference between old and new numerical delta values when performing a Delta Extraction, rather than just new measure values.

 

Support X509 certificate auth for OData-based Connections

Users can now select Client Certificate authentication and use an X.509 certificate for generic OData connections, SAP Business ByDesign Analytics and SAP S4/HANA Cloud connections.

 

Story Design

 

Optimized Story Experience now supports Export as Google Slides

The optimized story experience now supports export as Google Slides. Google Slides can be saved as a new presentation, or the slides can be added to an existing presentation in a Google Drive folder.

This is not supported in the lite viewer.

 

Lite Viewer now Supports Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension in Geographical Visualizations

The Lite Viewer now supports color by dimension for the bubble layer in geo maps. Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension will no longer cause an automatic redirect to the Full Viewer.

There are few improvements that have been made to the interactivity of Geographical Visualizations which includes:

  • Improved Interaction with Color by Dimension Bubble
  • Consolidation of Drill with Tooltip
  • Improved Threshold Tooltip

 

Story Extensibility

In the optimized story experience, dynamic text and variable related script APIs such as getVariables and getVariableValues now support non-input-ready variables.

Retrieve the variables that are not input ready, such as customer exit variables, from a live connection to SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW):

  • As Dynamic Text
  • Through script APIs
    • getDataSource().getVariableValues()
    • getDataSource().getVariables

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Dedicated OData Analytics APIs for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM offers a generic OData interface to access analytics of the differents use cases (Projects Management, Tasks Management, Health Monitoring...).

In addition to this generic OData interface, some use cases like "Requirements" provide a specific OData interface which is more convenient to use since it returns a dataset with the real name of dimensions and measures instead of generic names like d1k, d1v.

In SAP Analytics Clouds, you can consume the specific OData interface out-of-the-box by selecting the dedicated entity which is available using the same connection that you'd use to access the generic entity DataSet. Just select entity "Requirements" to access analytics of of SAP Cloud ALM Requirements scenario.

Depending on the scenario, you might have to filter some dimensions. In the case of Requirements all filters are optional which means you can just select all dimensions and measures you need and press "Next" to load all requirement analytics data in your model.

The frame time dimension "period" and "resolution" are still relevant with the specific interface and will impact the number of records returned. 

Once data imported in the SAP Analytics Cloud model you can manipulate the dimensions and measures using their real name as opposed to the generic DataSet interface which exposes generic dimension names (dxk,dxv) and generic measure names (mxk,mxv).

Although the format of generic OData interface and specific OData interface differ, they return the same content. 

References

  • SAP Cloud ALM Analytics API
  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub
  • Requirement analytics API
  • Access SAP Cloud ALM analytics from SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Access SAP Cloud ALM analytics catalog through OData interface

entry.title: 上新啦！SAP 分析云 2024.11 版功能更新
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能 (BI)、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。

本文介绍了 SAP 分析云 2024.11 版本的新功能。这些新功能已经在 SAP 分析云 FastTrack 客户的系统上线。对于 SAP 分析云季度发布周期 (QRC) 客户，此版本及其功能将作为 QRC 2024 年第3季度版本的一部分提供。在此处可以查看 QRC 发布时间表。 

系统和技术要求

尽管不是必需的，我们推荐您升级到最新的 SAP 分析云代理版本1.0.407，以利用所有数据采集类型。

代理的最低版本仍为 1.0.75，但以下情况除外：

  • SAP ERP/SQL 数据库的版本 1.0.99
  • SAP universe （UNX） 的版本 1.0.365（SAP 分析云代理需要其他设置步骤，有关详细信息，请参阅 SAP 说明 3262098）
  • SAP Business Warehouse （BW） 的版本 1.0.353
  • SAP HANA View 的版本 1.0.235
  • File Server版本 1.0.248

有关详细信息，请参阅系统要求和技术先决条件。

有关SAP BW与SAP分析云集成的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅SAP Note 2541557了解更多详细信息。

有关通过 SAP分析云的 SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 实时访问Universe时的支持功能和需求更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2771921了解更多详细信息。

请从“Service Marketplace”下载最新版本的简易部署工具包。

  • 注意：为避免下载旧版本，以及可能让用户面对Cloud Connector 与 SAPJVM 或 Apache Tomcat 的不当绑定问题，只有三个最新版本的工具包可供下载。

有关云代理版本变更管理详情，请参阅 SAP Note 3264839。

更新亮点

  • 管理和服务管理
    • 防止丢失通过 BTP 意外删除的 SAP 分析云租户
  • 数据发掘
    • 在数据分析器中为两个结构BW查询添加对结构成员的计算
    • 数据分析器中保留上次使用值
  • 数据集成
    • 数据导出 API – 增量差异
    • 支持基于 OData 连接的 X509 证书身份验证
  • 故事设计
    • 优化故事体验现在支持导出为 Google 幻灯片
    • 在地理可视化对象中，精简版查看器现在支持按维着色的气泡图层
  • 故事可扩展性
    • 以动态文本或脚本 API 的形式检索非输入就绪变量

 

管理和服务管理

防止丢失通过 BTP 意外删除的 SAP 分析云租户

如果您不小心在 SAP BTP 主控室中删除了服务实例，可在七天内恢复。点击此处此处了解更多信息。

 

数据发掘

在数据分析器中为两个结构BW查询添加对结构成员的计算

在“可用对象”面板的“结构”部分中增加了“+添加计算”按钮。这样就可以在 BW的两个结构化查询中使用结构成员创建计算。

 

数据分析器中保留上次使用值

现在，上次使用的已定义部件和交互值将被保留，在重新打开数据分析器时，选项将保持不变。数据分析器用户现在可以设置以下内容：

  • 默认显示/隐藏过滤器行
  • 分别打开/关闭样式面板和构建器面板和可用对象列表
  • 默认情况下，展开/折叠样式设置面板和生成器面板和可用对象列表
  • 默认情况下，在数据源浏览器中设置系统和连接类型
  • 保留 PDF、Microsoft Excel 和 CSV 导出对话框中的所有设置（导出名称除外）

 

数据集成

数据导出 API - 增量差异

数据导出服务 API 订阅已扩展，以支持在执行增量提取时计算新旧数值增量值之间的差异，而不仅仅是新度量值。

 

基于 OData 连接的 X509 证书身份验证

用户现在可以选择客户端证书身份验证，并将 X.509 证书用于通用 OData 连接、SAP Business ByDesign Analytics 和 SAP S4/HANA Cloud 连接。

 

故事设计

优化故事体验现在支持导出为 Google 幻灯片

优化故事体验现在支持导出为 Google 幻灯片。Google 幻灯片可以另存为新演示文稿，也可以添加到 Google Drive 文件夹现有演示文稿中。

精简查看器中不支持这一功能。

 

地理可视化对象中，精简版查看器现在支持按维着色的气泡图层

精简查看器现在支持地理地图中的气泡图层按维着色。按维着色的气泡图层将不再自动重新定向到完整查看器。

“地理可视化对象”的交互性也有一些改进：

对地理可视化的交互性进行了一些改进，其中包括：

  • 改进了与按维着色的气泡的交互
  • 使用工具提示合并钻取
  • 改进阈值工具提示

 

故事可扩展性

在优化故事体验中，动态文本和与变量相关的脚本 API（如 getVariables 和 getVariableValues）现在支持非输入就绪变量。

从与 SAP Business Warehouse （SAP BW） 的实时连接中检索未准备好输入的变量，例如客户出口变量：

  • 作为动态文本
  • 通过脚本 API
    • getDataSource（）.getVariableValues（）
    • getDataSource（）.getVariables

 

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和改进功能的高级概述。您可以在针对FastTrack客户的应用内帮助指南中找到有关这些功能的更多详细信息，如果您想了解更多关于FastTrack和季度发布周期发布的信息，请参阅这篇SAP 知识库文章。

如果您对本博文的内容有任何疑问，可以在下方发表评论。如果您有具体的产品或技术问题，请在我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 问答论坛上提问。访问我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 社区页面，了解更多产品信息、卓越实践等。查看我们的 SAP 路线图浏览器，了解 SAP Analytics Cloud 即将推出的更多功能。

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
The objective of this blog post is to help provide an overview to establish the live data connection between SAP Analytics Cloud and the SAP SuccessFactors system.  

With this new enhancement, you can copy the live connection (used in People Analytics Stories) to the Enterprise edition of SAP Analytics Cloud.

Some additional capabilities available to this live connection in SAC Enterprise are:

  • Data consolidation and cross application reporting

  • Use both Acquired and Live connections in a single story.

  • Build custom analytical applications. 

  • Scripting Capabilities

  • Custom Widgets/ Widget Add-ons

  • Composites

  • View stories in presentation mode

  • Theming capabilities

  • Public Models can be re-used.

 

Below is the architecture of the connection options available to an SAP SuccessFactors system.

Pre-requisites: 

  • You must be assigned the Story Admin permission in SAP SuccessFactors.

  • You must be assigned the BI Admin role in SAP Analytics Cloud to be able to copy the live connection to SAP Analytics Cloud.

  • You have enabled and configured Stories in People Analytics on your SAP SuccessFactors tenant.

  • You have a common Identity Authentication service between SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Analytics Cloud (for single sign on).

 
Creating the live connection in the SAP Analytics Cloud: 
  • From the side navigation, choose   Connections -> (Add Connection).  

The Select a data source dialog will appear.  

  • Expand Connect to Live Data and select SAP Cloud Application.  

  • You are now asked to Sign in, From the Select application dropdown pick SAP SuccessFactors.  

For Application URL enter your SAP SuccessFactors URL e.g.: https://xxx.sf.com.

Enter your Company ID, the Sign In button is activated, Click it.

  1. If SSO is enabled, once you select Sign In, you are signed in automatically.

If not, a Log on dialog appears to connect to SAP SuccessFactors. Provide user credentials to Log On.

The validation of your credentials and role can take up to a minute.

  1. You will now see a list of connections available.   Select it and click Next to copy it.

Currently “SAP SuccessFactors Reporting” connection is supported. In the subsequent release we will support “SAP SuccessFactors Learning” connection as well.

 Note: If this connection already exists in the tenant, an error message is displayed. Please delete the old connection and try again.

  1. Select the connection you want to clone and click Create.  

  7. The new connection details are displayed in the Clone Summary page.

Select Finish to complete the workflow.

Note: After copying the SuccessFactors connection, you must refresh the page.  

Tip: It is recommended that you use the same IAS (Identity Authentication Service) for both SuccessFactors and SAP Analytics Cloud for Single sign on else you will always be prompted for your credentials in all workflows.

Conclusion:  You have configured a successful live connection between SAP Analytics Cloud and the SAP SuccessFactors system.    

 

Using the Modeler

To create the first model, select Modeler from the side panel

Select “Live Data Model” option.

  • Select “SAP Cloud Application” in the System Type dropdown. Select “SAPSFSFREP” as the connection.

  • The credential for the connection is validated. Once confirmed the OK button is enabled.

  • The modeler provides a similar experience as in the Stories for People analytics with minor changes.

Some changes include cancel, preview query, finish buttons removed from the bottom. Preview button is moved to the top of tool bar.

  • Once the model is complete. You can save it in a folder to be used by Stories.

Note:  The Model saved as a public model in any accessible folder. This means you can now re-use the same model in multiple stories.

You can now design a story using this model.

Pro-Tip: you can design stories with highly aggregated widgets delivered via acquired connection, with a linked analysis to a table widget that is using the live connection to display employee level data.
This can be a important design consideration to use coarse grain Data Access Control in Datasphere, leverage the live connection and the fine-grained Role based Permission to control what a user can see on employee level.

 

Restrictions to SAP SuccessFactors Live Data Connections
  • Classic Design Experience
  • Scheduled Publication (Planned)
  • Data Change Insights
  • Model Repointing
  • Time Hierarchies
  • R Widgets
  • Search To Insights
  • Geo / Map Functionality
  • Natural Language Queries

All the restrictions applicable to SAP HANA live connections are also valid here.

entry.title: SAP Data and Analytics Forum 12.-13.11.2024
entry.content:
We look forward to welcome you on the 12th to 13th. November at this year’s german speaking SAP Data and Analytics Forum. An exciting program with inspiring lectures and hands-on sessions awaits you.  

There are two ways to participate in the event:  

Option 1: You only visit the first day, in virtual format  

The first day is free and virtual. Current trends and developments in Data and Analytics are discussed here. From best practices to new technologies and applications such as SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere, you will be able to gain valuable insights and expand your knowledge.  

Anticipate three parallel tracks:  

  1. SAP Analytics Cloud: Strategy & Roadmap SAP Analytics Cloud  
  2. SAP Datasphere: Strategy & Roadmap SAP Datasphere 
  3. Extended Planning & Analytics: Potentials and insights from practical projects. 

Sign up here for the first free and virtual day (preliminary agenda)  

Option 2: You will visit both days on site in Walldorf  

If you also prefer personal communication, please visit us on the 12. and 13.11 at the SAP training center in Walldorf. The second paid day offers you the opportunity to work directly on the system and to deepen your skills and experiences in hands-on sessions. You can get to know new tools and functions and discuss them directly with our experts.  

You can expect three parallel tracks:  

  1. Hands-on Analytics: Experience SAP Analytics Cloud in different scenarios. Try out the use cases and exercises created specifically for the Forum directly in the system. Get ready for exciting exercises that delve into the interaction between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere, as well as address connectivity with SAP Analytics Cloud. 
  2. Hands-on Data Warehousing: This track focuses on SAP Datasphere. You can use scenarios created specifically for you to get to know the product yourself in the system. Practical exercises that cover delta handling of loading processes or SAP BW bridge integration await you. 
  3.  Hands-on Advanced: You already have experience with SAP Data and Analytics solutions and would like to dive deeper into the matter? Then visit the sessions of the Advanced Track. There are advanced exercises in SAP Analytics Cloud focusing on performance in story design or on best practices in SAP Datasphere security. Prepare yourself for a captivating exercise that provides insights into an AI use case with SAP HANA Cloud. 

You can choose flexibly between the sessions of the different tracks and thus put together their personal agenda.   

Don’t miss out on asking questions directly to our product management experts and our SAP trainers.   

Sign up here for the SAP Data and Analytics Forum right here. (Preliminary Agenda)

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.12
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.12 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q3 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.407 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Software Center.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Tenant Links Tab Update
  • AI for Planning and Analytics
    • Just Ask: Prompt Guide
    • Just Ask: Display Dimension Attributes
  • Story Design
    • Apply default color palette to Table in Cell Charts
  • Story Extensibility
    • Custom Widget Enhancement: Incoporation of Unbooked Data
    • Apply Scaled Measures across All Included Pages in PDF/PPTX Exporting

 

Feature Category Naming Update

Before we dive in, you may have noticed a new feature category listed in the road map, and product plan. We have renamed “Augmented Analytics” as “AI for Planning and Analytics”.

 

Administration and Service Management

 

Tenant Links Tab Update

Administrators can configure a link between their SAP Analytics Cloud tenant and SAP Datasphere tenant. This configuration was previously on the "System Configuration" tab of the System / Administration page. This has now been moved to a new tab named "Tenant Links" on the same page. It was moved to highlight this feature and to provide room for future similar configurations. Among other connections this configuration will setup the App Switcher at the top right corner of SAP Analytics Cloud allowing end users to quickly link to other related products like SAP Datasphere.

 

AI for planning and analytics

 

Just Ask: Prompt Guide

A Prompt Guide is now available to provide users with guidance for best usage of Just Ask features. The guide includes:

  • Examples questions to query
  • Highlights of key features
  • Tips to optimize performance 

 

Just Ask: Display Dimension Attributes

Dimension attributes for models in Just Ask are now visible in the following:

  • When selecting the + button next to next to the search bar
  • Model Overview tab (in the Manage Model area)
  • Explore Data tab (in the Manage Model area)
  • In the Rules section

 

Story Design

 

Apply default color palette to Table in Cell Charts

In optimized story experience, the default color palette users can select for charts in the Theme Preferences is now also available to in-cell charts in tables. The bar or column charts in table cells are displayed in the first color of the selected palette. If you select a different color in the table builder panel, that color is used and any changes to the default color palette aren't applied to the in-cell charts of this table.

 

Story Extensibility

 

Custom Widget Enhancement: Incoporation of Unbooked Data

Story designers can now show or hide unbooked data in custom widgets.

 

Apply Scaled Measures across All Included Pages in PDF/PPTX Exporting

When exporting a story to a PDF to PPTX file, story viewers can now select Apply Scaled Measures Across All Included Pages so that the chart measures have the same scale across pages in the exported file. The option is off by default and only available when there is an active measure that is part of Chart Scaling.

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more.
